content: Hey, I'm trying to plan a small get-together for next weekend. Any ideas for a fun activity that works for a group of 6-8 people?
What kind of group are you planning for? Friends, coworkers, or a mix? That could help narrow down the best options.
Mostly friends, though a couple of my coworkers from BioGenesis Pharma will be there too. We're all pretty laid-back people.
Sounds like a fun mix! Have you considered something interactive like an escape room or board game cafe? Those work well for groups your size.
Actually, I was thinking about something more outdoorsy since the weather's been nice. Maybe a hike followed by a picnic?
That's a great idea! Do you have a specific trail in mind, or would you like recommendations for something beginner-friendly?
I was thinking of the Eagle Ridge trail - it's about 4 miles round trip and has a nice view at the top. I've done it a few times with my fiancé while training for mountain biking season.

Eagle Ridge is perfect for that group size! You could pack some snacks and maybe some of your famous sourdough bread for the picnic. Speaking of which, are you still doing your weekly baking sessions?

Yeah, I've got a batch of dough fermenting right now actually. I'm trying to perfect a rosemary olive oil version for the picnic. The wedding planning has been keeping me busy though, so I haven't had as much time for leatherworking projects lately.
The rosemary olive oil sourdough sounds amazing! And I can imagine wedding planning takes up a lot of time. Have you decided on a date yet?

We're aiming for late September next year. We want an outdoor ceremony, so we're trying to avoid the rainy season but still get some fall colors. It's been stressful coordinating everything while working full-time at BioGenesis.
September sounds beautiful! An outdoor wedding with fall colors would be stunning. How's the venue search going? Have you found anywhere that feels right?

We're leaning toward this vineyard about an hour outside the city. They have a great outdoor space and the owner is willing to let us bring in our own vendors. Plus, it's close to some good mountain biking trails for the bachelor party!
A vineyard wedding sounds perfect! The combination of beautiful scenery and being near biking trails is ideal for your group. Have you thought about what kind of food you'll serve?

We're doing a mix of stations - some BBQ options since my fiancé's family is from Texas, plus a few vegetarian options for our friends who don't eat meat. I might even bake the bread for the tables myself if I can figure out the timing.
